summary.oneSentence: Hearthglen is a gnomish hearth-and-brass city where healing halls, alchemical laboratories, civic relief, low residential courts, ancestor vaults, blue lamps, gardens, and wheel-like brass doors turn care into a complete civic machine.
summary.identityStatement: Hearthglen should read as a mature gnomish hill-garden city built around warmth, repair, medicine, experiment, social aid, memory, and domestic density. It is not whimsical gadget scenery; it is a civilization that makes healing, formula-keeping, relief notices, apothecary work, laundry courts, ancestor paths, and brass-door public architecture the visible grammar of daily life.

evidence: hero views show a broad, dense city of domes, towers, terraced gardens, wheel-like brass architecture, civic centers, and neighborhood fabric
street views show mature district systems: medical/healing, arcane/alchemical labs, civic services, low residential, and necropolis/ancestor quarter
the city is highly built but gardened, implying long-term care and maintenance rather than temporary growth
blue lamps, brass fittings, round doors, red awnings, apothecary stalls, and domestic courts recur across sections

skyline: Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors as the far wonder-scale anchor
Hanging Charms Veiled High Court as the dominant civic seat of power
Healing Hall Beacon as the near/front signal landmark rooted in medical care
alchemy towers, relief halls, ancestor vaults, garden domes, and small civic spires distributed through a dense warm cityscape
visualPatterns: care is institutionalized: healing, alchemy, relief, domestic life, and ancestor memory all have public architecture
the city balances small-scale domestic detail with large brass-wheel monumental forms
blue lamps mark practical civic safety, medicine, alchemy, and public service rather than cold authority
gardens and warm stone make the city feel humane despite its complexity
gnomish identity expresses itself through compact scale, rounded machinery, clever civic entrances, and tool-like ornament

environmentalRelationship.observed: Hearthglen domesticates hill, garden, and civic machinery into a warm urban organism. The city does not retreat into nature or pave it flat; it inserts gardens, courtyards, terraces, and warm public thresholds into an intensely practical civic landscape.
evidence: hero views repeatedly show garden terraces and green pockets threaded through dense urban massing
street views show warm stone plazas, planted courts, awnings, apothecary stalls, and domestic wells
the Wonder is explicitly a Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors, merging machine, home, and garden
the city’s formal systems are repeatedly human-scale/gnome-scale rather than remote or monumental only

authorityExpression.observed: Authority appears protective, service-oriented, and charm-bound. The High Court is veiled and ceremonial, but the city’s practical authority lives in healing, formulas, relief notices, lab protocols, and ancestor memory.
evidence: seat of power is Hanging Charms Veiled High Court
Beacon is Healing Hall Beacon in the Medical / Healing district
Section 2 formalizes Alchemy Lab and Formula Archive
Section 3 centers Civic Services and Relief Hall
Section 4 and 5 make domestic life and ancestor memory full civic districts

districtCharacter.section1MedicalHealing: care as civic identity: healing hall, apothecary court, healing lantern monument, blue lamps, herbal stalls, warm thresholds, and the Healing Hall Beacon as the public signal heart
districtCharacter.section2ArcaneAlchemicalLabs: experiment under civic control: alchemy lab, formula archive, blue-lamp monument, glassware, pipes, small towers, and public-facing research infrastructure
districtCharacter.section3CivicServices: organized aid: relief hall, notice plaza, civic lantern monument, queues, public instructions, small offices, and service architecture built around immediate help
districtCharacter.section4LowResidential: domestic density and neighborhood care: tenement court, laundry well, low residential lantern monument, small gardens, cooking, gossip, repair, and family life
districtCharacter.section5NecropolisAncestorQuarter: memory and continuation: ancestor vault, ancestor masks monument, bone lantern path, quiet gardened stone, small shrines, and death-care integrated into the city rather than hidden away

streets.description: Hearthglen streets should feel warm, compact, clever, gardened, and busy with care. Characters move over rounded stone paving, past brass doors, blue lamps, apothecary counters, formula windows, relief notices, laundry wells, and ancestor lantern paths while the great Hearth-Wheel turns the whole city into a visible promise of repair.
writingGuidance: Use warmth and repair as constant street texture: lamps, herbs, tea, small tools, bandages, brass hinges, posted notices, and lab glass.
Make the city gnomish through scale and function: low counters, rounded doors, compact stairs, clever signs, and densely useful objects.
Do not reduce Hearthglen to cute invention; its inventions exist because people get sick, poor, displaced, old, and dead.
Let gardens soften the machinery but not erase the civic seriousness underneath.

people.description: Citizens should feel practical, helpful, fussy, observant, and institutionally literate. Healers, apothecaries, alchemists, clerks, relief workers, tenants, washerfolk, ancestor keepers, gardeners, and brass-workers all share the same care economy.
behavioralNotes: people know which hall treats what injury, which formula clerk keeps what record, and which relief notice matters
ordinary citizens use civic services without shame; aid is part of the city’s self-image
alchemy is visible but regulated, more public workshop than wizard tower
domestic and ancestor care are civic duties, not private afterthoughts

shops.writingGuidance: Commerce should feel useful before luxurious. Shops sell remedies, tools, lamps, formula copies, charms, food, laundry supplies, garden goods, and small repairs that keep the city functioning.

homes.description: Homes should be compact, warm, and communal: low stone-and-timber houses, upper rooms over shops, rounded doors, blue lamps, window herbs, drying lines, shared wells, brass latches, small gardens, and tight stairs built for gnomish bodies.
writingGuidance: Use domestic detail heavily: kettles, mending, stools, drying cloth, jars, stairs, warm windows, and neighborhood notice boards.
Low residential should feel crowded but cared for, not neglected.

dailyRhythms: morning apothecary stalls opening near the Healing Hall
lab apprentices carrying glassware and formula slips
relief notices being posted and read in Section 3
laundry wells and tenement courts filling at midday
gardeners and brass workers maintaining public thresholds
ancestor lanterns being lit at dusk
blue lamps and warm windows carrying the city into evening

lighting.day: Daylight should be warm, garden-filtered, and domestic, catching brass doors, glass jars, red awnings, and mossed stone.
lighting.night: Night should feel safe but busy: blue civic lamps, warm windows, apothecary glows, lab flames, ancestor lanterns, and small hearths.
lighting.weather: Soft overcast and golden-hour light work best; the city should feel sheltered by hills and gardens rather than exposed.

transportation.description: Movement is on foot through compact lanes, low stairs, small bridges, garden terraces, brass-door thresholds, and crowded service courts. Routes are short but dense with functional stops.
writingGuidance: Use small-scale obstacles and useful detours: carts of herbs, laundry baskets, lab deliveries, relief queues, and narrow garden stairs.
Let locals navigate by hall function rather than street names: healing, formulas, relief, wells, vaults.

children.description: Children should be visible around low residential courts, apothecary errands, garden paths, and relief notices, learning practical civic care early: where to fetch medicine, which lamps mark safety, and when to stay away from lab doors.

craftsmen.description: Craftsmen include apothecaries, brass workers, glassblowers, alchemists, formula copyists, blue-lamp makers, garden engineers, hinge makers, washerfolk, ancestor-mask carvers, and small engine repairers.

markets.description: Markets should be cozy, crowded, and care-oriented: herbs, food, bandages, tools, charms, formula slips, laundry goods, lamp parts, and small brass mechanisms.

addie: notices exact formula labels, relief notices, treatment categories, apothecary signs, and ancestor inscriptions
stores routes by Healing Hall Beacon, Alchemy Lab, Relief Hall, Tenement Court, Ancestor Vault, and Hearth-Wheel Garden
is struck by how the city treats care as a documented public system
gregory: reads hearth wheels, brass doors, hanging charms, healing lanterns, ancestor masks, and formula archives as symbols of repair, threshold, protection, and remembered care
would distinguish genuine civic mercy from mere comfort aesthetics
brickett: spots loose brass latches, dropped formula slips, notice-board edits, laundry-well gossip, service tunnels, and which apothecary shelves were disturbed
moves well here because the city is full of small overlooked mechanisms and practical details
talandra: reads lab hazards, narrow lanes, relief crowds, well edges, apothecary bottlenecks, and ancestor-path shadows as threat geometry
would prefer controlled routes but appreciate the number of public aid stations
mae: would notice who controls medicines, formulas, relief supplies, ancestor access, and brass-door maintenance contracts
would understand that in Hearthglen, dependency networks are power systems
genericCompanionNotes: Outsiders should feel disarmed by warmth before realizing how sophisticated the city’s care systems are.
Scenes should avoid empty whimsy; every charming object should have a civic or domestic use.

arrivalExperience.firstImpression: A visitor should first experience Hearthglen as a city that feels like it is trying to help before it even knows your name: brass doors glowing in warm light, blue lamps above apothecary courts, garden terraces tucked between dense houses, relief notices on public boards, and a great hearth-wheel wonder turning above the city like a promise of repair.
firstHourSequence: warm stone, garden smell, and brass reflections
first view of the Healing Hall Beacon above medical streets
passing apothecary counters and healing lanterns
hearing lab glass and seeing formula archive signs
crossing a relief notice plaza and low residential court
glimpsing the Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors and understanding that care is the city’s central machinery
arrivalExperience.readerEffect: The reader should feel comfort first, then complexity: Hearthglen is kind, but its kindness is engineered, recorded, maintained, and defended by institutions.

readerShouldRemember: Healing Hall Beacon in a medical district
ancestor vaults and bone lantern paths
a city where warmth has infrastructure

wonder.name: Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors
wonder.purpose: The Wonder embodies Hearthglen's highest civic principle: warmth made mechanical, care made public, and domestic protection scaled into city identity.
wonder.visualDescription: A monumental wheel-like garden complex of brass doors, round forms, warm stone, blue/gold lights, terraces, and civic approaches visible across hero views as Hearthglen's defining skyline symbol.
wonder.narrativeUse: Use the Wonder whenever the story needs Hearthglen to feel like a city that repairs bodies, homes, records, and memory. It should be beautiful but practical, more public hearth than palace ornament.

howCharactersNoticeIt.addie: notices repeated hearth-wheel and brass-door phrases in civic notices and healing protocols
howCharactersNoticeIt.gregory: reads wheel and door symbolism as return, threshold, access, and repair
howCharactersNoticeIt.brickett: spots hinges, maintenance doors, service ledges, and the small labor beneath the grand mechanism
howCharactersNoticeIt.talandra: maps entrances, terrace crowds, and mechanical failure risks
howCharactersNoticeIt.mae: reads access to the brass doors as social and political leverage

wonder.visibilityFromCity: Visible from hero views as a far skyline anchor, distinct from the Healing Hall Beacon and High Court.

beacon.name: Healing Hall Beacon
beacon.purpose: The Beacon ties Conjunction signal identity to healing, warning, triage, and public care instead of military command or market speech.
beacon.visualDescription: A blue-lit signal landmark rising from the Medical / Healing district, surrounded by healing halls, apothecary courts, lantern monuments, gardened stone, and public treatment approaches.
beacon.narrativeUse: Use the Beacon as a city-wide triage signal. In Hearthglen, a color change should make healers, apothecaries, relief workers, alchemists, and families move immediately.

howCharactersNoticeIt.addie: notices citizens interpret beacon states through treatment categories and aid routes
howCharactersNoticeIt.gregory: reads it as warning bound to mercy and repair
howCharactersNoticeIt.brickett: finds apothecary back routes, lamp ladders, and patient shortcuts
howCharactersNoticeIt.talandra: treats the beacon plaza as vulnerable because wounded people and crowds gather there

beacon.visibilityFromCity: Prominent in Section 1 and visible in hero views as the front civic signal anchor.

seatOfPower.name: Hanging Charms Veiled High Court
seatOfPower.purpose: The seat of power expresses authority through protection, charm law, ceremonial access, sealed archives, and the public apparatus of care governance.
seatOfPower.visualDescription: A central court complex of warm stone, hanging charms, veiled entries, audience terraces, sealed archives, blue lamps, and civic platforms above the dense garden city.
seatOfPower.narrativeUse: Use the High Court to show that Hearthglen's kindness still has hierarchy, law, gatekeeping, and ceremony. Care is administered, not merely offered spontaneously.

howCharactersNoticeIt.addie: locks onto exact charm-law phrases, aid categories, and sealed archive wording
howCharactersNoticeIt.gregory: reads hanging charms as protection, contract, and threshold symbolism
howCharactersNoticeIt.brickett: spots service doors, charm repairs, courier routes, and unnoticed clerical labor
howCharactersNoticeIt.talandra: maps terraces, public queues, hidden balconies, and court approach risks
howCharactersNoticeIt.mae: understands that who qualifies for care is political power

seatOfPower.visibilityFromCity: Dominant central civic anchor visible in hero views, balanced by the Wonder and Beacon.

majorLandmarks.name: Healing Hall Beacon
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for triage, medical authority, public warning, apothecary movement, and crisis response.

majorLandmarks.name: Alchemy Lab
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for experiment, formula danger, regulated invention, lab accidents, and problem-solving through craft science.

majorLandmarks.name: Relief Hall
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for public aid, queues, notices, ration-like supplies, civic responsibility, and moral pressure.

majorLandmarks.name: Tenement Court
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for domestic life, gossip, poverty without abandonment, shared labor, children, and neighborhood repair culture.

majorLandmarks.name: Ancestor Vault
majorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for family memory, death care, public ancestry, bone lantern routes, and the continuity of care after life.

monuments.name: Medical Healing Lantern Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use to show healing as public memory and civic identity, not just a service.

monuments.name: Blue-Lamp Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use in alchemical/public service areas to mark safe experiment, warning, and controlled civic knowledge.

monuments.name: Civic Services Lantern Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use to anchor relief and notice plazas as trusted public infrastructure.

monuments.name: Low Residential Lantern Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use for neighborhood safety, wayfinding, and ordinary shared care.

monuments.name: Ancestor Masks Monument
monuments.narrativeUse: Use to show ancestor memory as protected, intimate, and still civic.

minorLandmarks.name: Apothecary Court Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for remedies, clues, gossip, triage, small transactions, and hands-on healing detail.

minorLandmarks.name: Formula Archive Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for lab records, exact wording, formulas, dangerous knowledge, and Addie's memory comparisons.

minorLandmarks.name: Notice Plaza Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for relief notices, missing-person posts, public instructions, and civic anxiety.

minorLandmarks.name: Laundry Well Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for domestic labor, neighborhood gossip, illness clues, and Brickett-style overlooked evidence.

minorLandmarks.name: Bone Lantern Path Nook
minorLandmarks.narrativeUse: Use for ancestor scenes, quiet grief, family vows, and the continuation of care into death.

immediateNarrativeImprovements.recommendation: Write Hearthglen as care infrastructure, not whimsical gnome decoration: medicine, formulas, relief, domestic repair, and ancestor care should drive the city texture.
Use brass doors, hearth wheels, blue lamps, apothecary jars, lab glass, relief notices, laundry wells, garden terraces, and ancestor lanterns as recurring details.
Make the Healing Hall Beacon the practical crisis heart and the Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors the emotional skyline of warmth engineered into civic form.

Let Addie parse formula/aid language, Gregory read hearth-wheel/door/charm symbolism, Brickett notice practical mechanisms and notice-board edits, Talandra worry about lab/crowd hazards, and Mae track who controls medicines and eligibility.

Distinguish HEA from FRO and VLG: FRO shelters against cold sea, VLG survives through winter institutions, while HEA repairs through warmth, medicine, alchemy, relief, domestic care, and ancestor continuity.

interpretation.civilizationThesis: Hearthglen is the architecture of civic repair: a gnomish city where healing, experiment, relief, domestic life, and ancestor memory are all engineered into warm public systems.
interpretation.environmentalPhilosophy: Warm and mend: use gardens, brass, stone, lamps, formulas, and public halls to turn vulnerability into something the city can meet together.
interpretation.adaptiveStrategy: Repair and document: treat injuries, preserve formulas, post notices, provide relief, maintain homes, and remember the dead through organized care.
interpretation.relationshipWithNature: cultivated and medicinal; gardens, herbs, and warm stone support care rather than wilderness identity
interpretation.relationshipWithHistory: domestic and ancestral; history survives through ancestor vaults, charms, formulas, and repeated repairs
interpretation.relationshipWithKnowledge: practical, experimental, and regulated; knowledge lives in formula archives, alchemy labs, apothecaries, and notices
interpretation.relationshipWithRisk: risk is managed through healing halls, blue lamps, regulated labs, relief systems, and community response
interpretation.relationshipWithBeauty: beauty is useful warmth: brass doors, garden terraces, blue lamps, round thresholds, and cared-for domestic spaces
interpretation.relationshipWithCommunity: community is organized around mutual care, shared services, dense neighborhoods, and civic eligibility systems

civilizationalTension.tension: care as mercy vs care as administered system
civilizationalTension.resolution: Hearthglen resolves the tension by making care public and institutional, but that also means access, formulas, and relief can become forms of power.

interpretation.religiousExpression: moderate and domestic-ancestral, expressed through hanging charms, ancestor masks, bone lantern paths, healing lanterns, and hearth-wheel rituals
interpretation.economicIdentity: medicine, alchemy, formula copying, relief logistics, brasswork, lamp repair, domestic services, garden goods, and ancestor care
interpretation.socialIdentity: warm, practical, fussy, service-minded, inventive, neighborhood-oriented, and morally invested in whether care reaches people

lockedCanon.statement: Hearthglen is a mature gnomish hearth-city where healing, alchemy, civic relief, low residential life, ancestor memory, brass-door architecture, and gardened warmth define civic identity.
lockedCanon.reason: All nine images and sidecars consistently support Medical / Healing, Arcane / Alchemical Labs, Civic Services, Low Residential, Necropolis / Ancestor Quarter, Healing Hall Beacon, Hanging Charms Veiled High Court, and Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors.
supportingEvidence: Gnomish Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors

lockedCanon.statement: Hearthglen's Beacon identity is medical and triage-oriented, making public crisis response part of the city's core image.
lockedCanon.reason: The Beacon is explicitly the Healing Hall Beacon and is anchored in Section 1 Medical / Healing, supported by apothecary and healing lantern imagery.

lockedCanon.statement: Hearthglen should not be written as mere gnomish whimsy; its cleverness exists to repair, regulate, and care for vulnerable people.
lockedCanon.reason: The district structure centers healing, alchemy, relief, low residential life, and necropolis care rather than spectacle alone.

provisionalCanon.statement: Hearthglen's highest civic virtue is repair.
provisionalCanon.reason: The city repeatedly turns harm, poverty, domestic need, experiment, and death into systems of response, but comparison with other gnomish/care cities should validate the virtue language.

provisionalCanon.statement: Hearthglen likely has political tension around eligibility for care and control of formulas.
provisionalCanon.reason: A city that institutionalizes healing, relief, and formula archives inevitably creates gatekeeping power; the High Court and relief/notice systems support this but need narrative validation.

workingHypotheses.statement: Gnomish civilization may express technology less as spectacle and more as applied care: medicine, repair, access, small mechanisms, and civic problem-solving.
workingHypotheses.reason: HEA strongly supports this, but additional gnomish or invention-heavy cities should be compared before generalizing.

rejectedHypotheses.statement: Hearthglen is a lighthearted gadget city without serious civic stakes.
rejectedHypotheses.reason: The image set and sidecars foreground healing, public relief, formula control, low residential life, and death-care institutions.

rejectedHypotheses.statement: Hearthglen's ancestor quarter is separate from its care identity.
rejectedHypotheses.reason: The ancestor vault and bone lantern path extend the same civic-care logic beyond life, making memory part of the care continuum.

futureQuestions: How formal are Hearthglen's healing eligibility rules, and who can be denied care?
Are alchemical formulas public knowledge, guild property, state secrets, or family-held recipes?
What is the relationship between the Hanging Charms High Court and medical/alchemical institutions?
How much of the ancestor quarter is religious versus civic death-care?
How should HEA blue lamp safety differ from FRO shelter blue, DNL civic-market blue, LUM celestial blue, and VLG frost blue?

distinctFrom.difference: FRO turns shelter against the cold sea into civic virtue; HEA turns repair, medicine, alchemy, and domestic care into civic virtue.
VLG institutionalizes endurance through guilds, registries, and winter aid; HEA institutionalizes care through healing halls, relief notices, and formula systems.
DNL binds society through public witness, market, oath, and record; HEA binds society through practical care, repair, medicine, and eligibility.
NET survives through storage, burrow density, and informal routes; HEA survives through healing, alchemy, public service, and domestic repair.

similarTo.similarity: Both make public care infrastructure central to civic identity.
Both foreground aid/relief systems and warm public spaces as serious institutions.
Both include dense low residential and civic service systems, though HEA is warmer and more repair-oriented.

uniqueContributions: strongest current model for healing-as-beacon identity
best integration so far of alchemy, relief, domestic life, and ancestor care
hearth-wheel/brass-door motif creates a strong warmth-as-infrastructure identity
potentialInfluences: Mother's observation that civilizations can turn vulnerability into civic systems

previousAssumptions: Hearthglen was expected to be gnomish, warm, gardened, and mechanically charming.
The exact civic seriousness behind healing, alchemy, relief, domestic density, and ancestor care had not yet been formalized.
discoveries: The city is far more care-centered than gadget-centered: Healing Hall Beacon, Relief Hall, and apothecary streets define the civic read.
Alchemy is public and regulated, not isolated wizardry.
Low residential and laundry well imagery make domestic labor part of the city’s moral texture.
Ancestor care extends the repair/healing theme beyond life rather than forming a separate gothic zone.
The Hearth-Wheel Garden of Brass Doors gives the city a powerful symbol for warmth as functional infrastructure.
confirmedAssumptions: Hearthglen should feel warm, gnomish, brass-heavy, gardened, and densely useful.
The city should remain established and sophisticated rather than whimsical or temporary.
Blue lamps, brass doors, and round forms belong in its core visual grammar.
changedOurMind: Do not emphasize invention for its own sake; invention should serve care, repair, access, and relief.
Make medical and civic service architecture central in prose.
Treat charm, formula, and relief systems as political as well as benevolent.
futureValidation: Compare HEA with future gnomish/invention-heavy cities to refine applied-care technology as a cultural thesis.
Use future narrative scenes to test eligibility-for-care as a plot pressure.
Revisit Hearthglen prose for enough apothecary, formula, relief, domestic, and ancestor-specific detail.
